mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal yields an out-of-sample Sharpe ratio greater than 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a statistically significant positive return differential compared to periods with divergent series movements at the 90% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during easing cycles, producing negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 55% of high-inflation trigger months or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls and the federal funds rate indicate an overheating economy facing tightening financial conditions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in more than 60% of triggered months or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly retail sales growth reflects immediate consumer spending strength, which translates into positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations during recovery phases or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.
